companion_files.name: PLAN.md
companion_files.content:
# Plan - Video.js Maturity

## Document Status

- Last updated: 2026-02-21
- Track: Current follow-up for segmented playback maturity hardening
- Source feature: `segmented-streaming-handoff-architecture`
- Status: Complete

## Why Now

1. Segmented playback is running in production and now needs maturity hardening for reliability and observability.
2. Current user-reported startup/offset issues benefit from deeper Video.js/VHS diagnostics and stricter runtime checks.
3. DASH compatibility and long-start edge cases need explicit evaluation paths and operational guardrails.

## Scope

1. Bring Video.js segmented playback implementation closer to mature production patterns.
2. Expand diagnostics and automated regression coverage for startup/handoff/buffering reliability.
3. Evaluate whether a dash.js-backed integration path is warranted for DASH edge-case handling.

## Current Tasks

### Task VJ1: Add VHS response telemetry + runtime stats capture
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: per-player response hook instrumentation and structured metrics for manifest/segment timing, error code, and throughput context.
- Notes: include `player.tech().vhs.stats`, `vhs.bandwidth`, and `vhs.throughput` snapshots and correlate with existing segmented client metric events.
- Completion summary (2026-02-21): Added `onResponse`-based VHS telemetry and sampled runtime stat snapshots in `frontend/lib/audio-engine/videoJsSegmentedEngine.ts`; verified with targeted frontend lint and frontend build.

### Task VJ2: Add VHS usage-event diagnostics
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: usage-event logging/metric forwarding for high-signal VHS runtime events (for example gap-skip, stalled/unknown waiting).
- Notes: ensure events are bounded and sampled to avoid excessive log volume.
- Completion summary (2026-02-21): Added player/tech VHS `usage` telemetry capture for high-signal events with per-track occurrence caps and runtime context fields in `frontend/lib/audio-engine/videoJsSegmentedEngine.ts`; verified via targeted frontend lint and full frontend build.

### Task VJ3: Implement ABR/quality-level observability and control path
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: quality-level event visibility plus explicit policy for manual quality pinning vs auto ABR during segmented playback.
- Notes: validate alignment with user playback-quality settings semantics.
- Completion summary (2026-02-21): Added Video.js quality-level observability (`snapshot`, `addqualitylevel`, `change`) and explicit ABR policy telemetry (`auto` mode with server-selected segmented quality and no client-side manual pinning) in `frontend/lib/audio-engine/videoJsSegmentedEngine.ts`; verified with targeted frontend lint and full frontend build.

### Task VJ4: Tune VHS startup/retry configuration
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: documented and tested VHS option profile tuned for faster startup and fewer rebuffer regressions.
- Notes: include rollback-safe defaults and runtime/feature-flag control where needed.
- Completion summary (2026-02-21): Added runtime-configured VHS profile control (`SEGMENTED_VHS_PROFILE`: `balanced` default, `legacy` rollback) with explicit startup-config telemetry and balanced startup/retry defaults in `frontend/lib/audio-engine/videoJsSegmentedEngine.ts`; wired runtime exposure in `frontend/app/runtime-config/route.ts` and documented env behavior in `docs/ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES.md`; verified with targeted frontend lint and full frontend build.

### Task VJ5: Decide audio-first runtime mode strategy
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: engineering decision for current hidden-video strategy vs explicit audio-only mode strategy, with browser compatibility notes.
- Notes: explicitly evaluate Video.js `audioOnlyMode` / `audioPosterMode` (and `<audio>` tech viability) vs current hidden-video behavior; capture decision and rationale in architecture notes.
- Completion summary (2026-02-21): Adopted Video.js `audioOnlyMode` when supported (with automatic hidden-video fallback when unavailable) and added runtime compatibility telemetry (`player.videojs_audio_mode`) to make audio-mode behavior explicit per client; decision keeps headless runtime element strategy while preferring native audio-mode semantics in supported browsers.

### Task VJ6: Add automated segmented Video.js regression tests
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: tests covering direct->segmented handoff, startup fallback, buffering recovery, and seek continuity.
- Notes: include at least one regression test for stale-time jump prevention.
- Completion summary (2026-02-21): Added `frontend/lib/audio-engine/segmentedPlaybackRegressionPolicy.ts` and wired `AudioPlaybackOrchestrator` to use shared deterministic helpers for trusted-position selection, segmented-startup fallback gating, buffering recovery action selection, and seek tolerance checks; added regression coverage in `frontend/tests/unit/segmentedPlaybackRegressionPolicy.test.ts` for direct->segmented handoff eligibility, startup fallback gating, buffering recovery policy, seek continuity tolerance, and stale-time jump prevention; verified with targeted frontend lint, targeted unit tests, and full frontend build.

### Task VJ7: Add media-contract predeploy checks
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: validation checks for byte-range support, CORS headers, content-type correctness, and fast-start metadata placement on manifest/segment/direct media paths.
- Notes: include explicit range-request/`206` verification and host capability checks; wire into existing predeploy validation path where feasible.
- Completion summary (2026-02-21): Added `frontend/tests/e2e/predeploy/media-contract.spec.ts` to assert direct-stream and segmented-stream contract behavior (explicit `Range` + `206`, `Accept-Ranges`/`Content-Range`, CORS response headers, and manifest/segment/direct content-types), including MP4 fast-start `moov` sanity checks when direct responses are MP4-family; wired this into the existing `test:predeploy` path by virtue of the `predeploy/*.spec.ts` suite and documented the check in `docs/TESTING.md`.

### Task VJ8: Spike `videojs-contrib-dash` (dash.js-backed) for DASH edge cases
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: comparison spike and recommendation memo covering:
  - current VHS path behavior in observed edge cases,
  - dash.js-backed behavior under the same scenarios,
  - integration complexity and maintenance cost,
  - go/no-go recommendation.
- Notes: this task is evaluation-only unless explicitly approved for implementation.
- Completion summary (2026-02-21): Produced comparison and recommendation memo in `docs/VIDEOJS_CONTRIB_DASH_SPIKE.md`; recommendation is to keep VHS as default and defer `videojs-contrib-dash` adoption unless reproducible DASH-specific failures remain after current orchestration/transcode stabilization or a dash.js-only tuning control is proven necessary, in which case a runtime-selectable canary with telemetry parity is advised.

## External References

1. https://github.com/videojs/http-streaming
2. https://videojs.org/guides/options/
3. https://videojs.org/guides/troubleshooting/
4. https://videojs.org/guides/faqs/
5. https://github.com/videojs/videojs-contrib-quality-levels
6. https://github.com/videojs/videojs-contrib-quality-menu
7. https://github.com/videojs/videojs-contrib-dash
8. https://dashif.org/dash.js/pages/usage/buffer-management.html

## Exit Criteria

1. All current tasks have explicit outcome status (`Complete`, `Rejected`, or `Deferred with reason`) in this plan.
2. Playback startup/handoff reliability metrics demonstrate improvement without regression to direct-stream baseline behavior.
3. DASH implementation-path decision (`VHS only` vs `VHS + dash.js fallback`) is documented with evidence and an operational rollout recommendation.
